name: wbml
documentation:
# [WBML](https://github.com/wesselb/wbml)

[![CI](https://github.com/wesselb/wbml/workflows/CI/badge.svg?branch=master)](https://github.com/wesselb/wbml/actions?query=workflow%3ACI)
[![Coverage Status](https://coveralls.io/repos/github/wesselb/wbml/badge.svg?branch=master&service=github)](https://coveralls.io/github/wesselb/wbml?branch=master)
[![Latest Docs](https://img.shields.io/badge/docs-latest-blue.svg)](https://wesselb.github.io/wbml)
[![Code style: black](https://img.shields.io/badge/code%20style-black-000000.svg)](https://github.com/psf/black)

Machine learning stuff

## Requirements and Installation

See [the instructions here](https://gist.github.com/wesselb/4b44bf87f3789425f96e26c4308d0adc).
Then simply

```
pip install wbml
```

functions.name: wbml.warning.warn_upmodule
documentation:
Call {func}`warnings.warn`, but set the keyword argument `stacklevel`
corresponding to the first other module above the current module.

classes.name: tests.lmm.TrackedIterator

methods.name: tests.lmm.TrackedIterator.reset
documentation: Reset all.

An iterator that keeps track of whether it has been used.

Attributes:
    used (bool): Boolean indicating whether it has been usd.

Args:
    wrap (object): Object to wrap.

functions.name: wbml.vi.elbo
documentation:
Construct the ELBO.

Args:
    lik (function): Likelihood that taken in one or more samples from the
        approximate posterior.
    p (distribution): Prior.
    q (distribution): Approximate posterior.
    num_samples (int, optional): Number of samples. Defaults to `1`.

Returns:
    tensor: ELBO.

functions.name: wbml.experiment.generate_root
documentation:
Generate a root path.

Args:
    name (str): Name of the experiment.

Returns:
    str: Root path.

classes.name: wbml.experiment.Logger

methods.name: wbml.experiment.Logger.write
documentation:
Write to the stream.

Args:
    message (str): Message to write.

methods.name: wbml.experiment.Logger.flush
documentation: Flush the stream.

A logger to a file. Open the file every time a message is written.

Args:
    path (str): Path to file.

classes.name: wbml.experiment.MultiStream

methods.name: wbml.experiment.MultiStream.write
documentation:
Write to the stream.

Args:
    message (str): Message to write.

methods.name: wbml.experiment.MultiStream.flush
documentation: Flush the stream.

Combine multiple streams into one.

Args:
    *streams (stream): Streams.

classes.name: wbml.experiment.WorkingDirectory

methods.name: wbml.experiment.WorkingDirectory.file
documentation:
Get the path of a file.

Args:
    *name (str): Path to file, relative to the root directory. Use
        different arguments for directories.
    exists (bool): Assert that the file already exists. Defaults to
        `False`.

Returns:
    str: Path to file.

methods.name: wbml.experiment.WorkingDirectory.save
documentation:
Save an object to a file.

Further takes in arguments and keyword arguments from
{meth}`.experiment.WorkingDirectory.file`.

Args:

: obj (object): Object to save.

methods.name: wbml.experiment.WorkingDirectory.load
documentation:
Load an object from a file.

Further takes in arguments and keyword arguments from
{meth}`.experiment.WorkingDirectory.file`.

Working directory.

Args:
    *root (str): Root of working directory. Use different arguments for
        directories.
    observe (bool, optional): Observe the results. Only set the random seed and do
        not do anything else. Defaults to `False.`
    override (bool, optional): Delete working directory if it already exists.
        Defaults to `False`.
    log (str or None, optional): Log. Set to `None` to disable. Defaults to
        `log.txt`.
    diff (str or None, optional): Record the output of `git diff`. Set to `None`
        to disable. Defaults to `diff.txt`.
    seed (int, optional): Value of random seed. Defaults to `0`.

classes.name: tests.test_out.RecordingStream
documentation: A stream that records its writes.

classes.name: tests.test_out.Mock
documentation:
Mock the stream that `wbml.out` uses. Also reset
`wbml.out._time_start`.

functions.name: wbml.data.vix.load
documentation:
VIX data.

Source:

: The history starting at 2 Jan 1990 until the time of download is downloaded
  from the following link:

  > <https://cdn.cboe.com/api/global/us_indices/daily_prices/VIX_History.csv>

Returns:

: {class}`pd.DataFrame`: VIX data.

functions.name: wbml.data.predprey.load
documentation:
Predator–prey data.

Source:
: The data is a digitised version of the historical data by D. R. Hundley:

  : <http://people.whitman.edu/~hundledr/courses/M250F03/LynxHare.txt>

Returns:

: {class}`pd.DataFrame`: Predator–prey data.

functions.name: wbml.data.eeg.load
documentation:
Load the EEG data, which is a particular split of the first trial of subject
337 in the large training data.

Args:
: extended (bool, optional): Return the train test splits of the first

  : train of all patients in the large training data. Defaults to `False`.

Returns:
: tuple\[{class}`pd.DataFrame`\] or dict\[int, {class}`pd.DataFrame`\]:

  : All data, training data, and test data of the first trail of one
    patient or all patients.

functions.name: wbml.data.eeg.load_full
documentation:
Load the full EEG data.

Returns:
    dict: Full data.

functions.name: wbml.data.eeg._fetch_large
documentation: Fetch the large training and test data set.

functions.name: wbml.data.eeg._fetch_full
documentation: Fetch the full data set.

functions.name: wbml.data.data.resource
documentation:
Specify a dependency on an online resource.

Further takes in keyword arguments that are passed to the appropriate method
from {mod}`requests` or {mod}`urllib`.

Args:

: target (str): Target file.
  url (str): Source URL.
  post (bool, optional): Make a POST request instead of a GET request.

  > Only applicable if the URL starts with "http" or "https". Defaults
  > to `False`.

functions.name: wbml.data.data.dependency
documentation:
Specify a dependency that is generated from an existing file.

Args:
    target (str): Target file.
    source (str): Source file.
    commands (list[str]): List of commands to generate target file.

functions.name: wbml.data.data.asserted_dependency
documentation:
Specify a dependency that cannot be fetched.

Args:
    target (str): Target file.

functions.name: wbml.data.data.make_dirs
documentation:
Make the directories in the path of a file.

Args:
    path (url): Path of a file.

functions.name: wbml.data.data.data_path
documentation:
Get the path of a data file.

Args:
    *xs (str): Parts of the path.

Returns:
    str: Absolute path.

functions.name: wbml.data.data.split_df
documentation:
Split a data frame by selecting from columns a particular range.

Args:

: df ({class}`pd.DataFrame`): Data frame to split.
  index_range (tuple): Tuple containing lower and upper limit of the

  > range to split the index by. If `index_range = (a, b)`, then
  > `[a, b)` is taken.

  columns (list[object]): Columns to select.
  iloc (bool, optional): The index range is the integer location instead

  > of the index value. Defaults to `False`.

Returns:
: tuple\[{class}`pd.DataFrame`\]: Selected rows from selected columns

  : and the remainder.

functions.name: wbml.data.data.date_to_decimal_year
documentation:
Convert a date to decimal year.

Args:
    date (str): Date as a string.
    format (str, optional): Format of the date if a conversion is needed.

Returns:
    float: Decimal year corresponding to the date.

classes.name: wbml.data.data.DependencyError
documentation: Exception raised in case of an erroneous dependency.

functions.name: wbml.data.exchange.load
documentation:
Load the data.

Source:

: <https://fx.sauder.ubc.ca/data.html>

Args:

: extended (bool, optional): Also return years 2000 to 2010. Defaults

  : to `False`.

  nguyen (bool, optional): Return the split from Nguyen & Bonilla (2014).

Returns:
: tuple\[{class}`pd.DataFrame`\]: Three-tuple or four-tuple containing

  : all the data, training data, test data, and potentially years
    2000 to 2010.

functions.name: wbml.data.air_temp.load
documentation:
Load air temperature data.

Args:
    split (str, optional): Name of split. Must be `None` or `"requeima19"`.

Returns:
    object: Loaded data. Depends on the value for `split`.

functions.name: wbml.data.kemar.load
documentation:
HRIRs from subject 21 from the standard KEMAR data set.

Source:
    https://www.ece.ucdavis.edu/cipic/spatial-sound/hrtf-data/

Returns:
    dict: Left and right HRIRs from subject 21.

functions.name: wbml.data.crude_oil.load
documentation:
Crude oil data.

Source:

: The historical data at the maximum window was downloaded at 9 Oct 2021 from the
  following link:

  > <https://www.nasdaq.com/market-activity/commodities/cl:nmx/historical>

Returns:

: {class}`pd.DataFrame`: Crude oil data.

functions.name: wbml.util.indented_kv
documentation:
Print something as a key-value pair whilst properly indenting. This is useful
    for implementations of`str` and `repr`.

    Args:
        key (str): Key.
        value (str): Value.
        indent (int, optional): Number of spaces to indent. Defaults to 1.
        separator (str, optional): Separator between the key and value. Defaults to "=".
        suffix (str, optional): Extra to print at the end. You can set this, e.g., to
            ",
" or ">". Defaults to no suffix.

    Returns
        str: Key-value representation with proper indentation.

functions.name: wbml.util.warn_upmodule
documentation:
Call {func}`warnings.warn`, but set the keyword argument `stacklevel`
corresponding to the first other module above the current module.

functions.name: wbml.util.inv_perm
documentation:
Invert a permutation.

Args:
    perm (list): Permutation to invert.

Returns:
    list: Inverse permutation.

functions.name: wbml.util.normal1d_logpdf
documentation:
Broadcast the one-dimensional normal logpdf.

Args:
    x (tensor): Point to evaluate at.
    var (tensor): Variances.
    mean (tensor): Means.

Returns:
    tensor: Logpdf.

classes.name: wbml.util.BatchVars

methods.name: wbml.util.BatchVars.get
documentation:
Get a batch of tensor of a particular shape.

Args:
    shape (shape): Shape of tensor.

Returns:
    tensor: Batch of tensors of shape `shape`.

Extract variables from a source with a batch axis as the first axis.

Args:
    source (tensor): Source.

modules.name: wbml._version
documentation: file generated by setuptools_scm
don't change, don't track in version control

documentation:
Mean squared error.

functions.name: wbml.metric.smse
documentation:
Standardised mean squared error.

Args:
    mean (tensor): Mean of prediction.
    data (tensor): Reference data.

Returns:
    tensor: Standardised mean squared error.

documentation:
Root mean squared error.

Standardised root mean squared error.

functions.name: wbml.metric.mae
documentation:
Mean absolute error.

documentation:
Mean log loss.

Standardised mean log loss.

functions.name: wbml.metric.r2
documentation:
R-squared.

Args:
    mean (tensor): Mean of prediction.
    data (tensor): Reference data.

Returns:
    tensor: R-squared.

functions.name: wbml.out._print
documentation:
Print a line with proper indentation.

    Args:
        msg (str): Message to print.
        line_end (str, optional): String to print at the end of the line.
            Defaults to "
".
        indent (bool, optional): Insert indentation. Defaults to `True`.
        flush (bool, optional): Flush after writing. Defaults to `False`.

functions.name: wbml.out.out
documentation:
Print a message.

Args:
    msg (str): Message to print.

functions.name: wbml.out.kv
documentation:
Print a key-value pair.

Args:
    key (str): Key.
    value (object): Value.
    fmt (str, optional): Format for the value. Defaults to automatic
        formatting.
    unit (str, optional): Unit of the value. Defaults to no unit.

functions.name: wbml.out.format
documentation:
Format an object.

Args:
    x (object): Object to format.
    info (bool, optional): Print additional info, such as the shape of an
        object, where appropriate. Defaults to `True`.

Returns:
    str: `x` as a string.

functions.name: wbml.out._compute_alpha
documentation:
Compute the coefficient `a` of the one-pole filter
`y[n] = a x[n] + (1 - a) x[n - 1]`.

Args:
    cutoff_lag (int): Cut-off frequency, in number of lags.

Returns:
    float: Coefficient `a`.

documentation: : Indentation level.

variables.name: wbml.out.indent_width
documentation: : Number of indentation characters per level.

documentation: : Indentation character.

documentation: : Minimum width for printing keys, for alignment.

classes.name: wbml.out._StdOut

methods.name: wbml.out._StdOut.write
documentation:
Write to the stream.

Args:
    message (str): Message to write.

methods.name: wbml.out._StdOut.flush
documentation: Flush the stream.

A stream which writes to {obj}`sys.stdout`. This is useful in the case that
the value of {obj}`sys.stdout` can change.

classes.name: wbml.out.Section
documentation:
Create a section in which output is indented.

Args:
    name (str): Name of the section.

classes.name: wbml.out.Counter

methods.name: wbml.out.Counter.count
documentation: Count once.

methods.name: wbml.out.Counter.map
documentation:
Perform a mapping operation that is counted.

Args:
    f (function): Function to apply.
    xs (iterable): Arguments to apply.
    name (str, optional): Name of the mapping operation. Defaults to
        "Mapping".

Returns:
    list: Result.

A counter.

Args:
    name (str, optional): Name of the counter. Defaults to "Counting".
    total (int, optional): Total number of counts. Defaults to no total.

classes.name: wbml.out.Progress

methods.name: wbml.out.Progress.report
documentation: Show a report.

methods.name: wbml.out.Progress.map
documentation:
Perform a mapping operation whose progress is shown.

Args:
    f (function): Function to apply.
    xs (iterable): Arguments to apply.
    name (str, optional): Name of the mapping operation. Defaults to
        "Mapping".

Returns:
    list: Result.

Display progress.

Args:
    name (str): Name of operation. Defaults to "Progress".
    total (int): Total number of iterations. Defaults to no total.
    filter (dict): A dictionary mapping names of recorded values to the
        cut-off frequency of a one-pole filter, in number of lags.
    interval (int or float): Interval at which to print a report in
        either number of seconds (float) or number of iterations (int).
        Defaults to one second.
    filter_global (int): Cut-off frequency of a one-pole filter of the
        default smoother, in number of lags.

functions.name: wbml.plot.patch
documentation:
Decorator to patch a function to automatically convert arguments that
are of a framework type, like PyTorch, to NumPy. Also allows a keyword
argument `style` to automatically expand the dictionary given by
{func}`.plot.style` into the keyword arguments.

functions.name: wbml.plot.tex
documentation: Use TeX for rendering.

functions.name: wbml.plot.tweak
documentation:
Tweak a plot.

Args:
    grid (bool, optional): Show grid. Defaults to `True`.
    legend (bool, optional): Show legend. Automatically shows a legend if any labels
        are set.
    legend_loc (str, optional): Position of the legend. Defaults to
        "upper right".
    spines (bool, optional): Hide top and right spine. Defaults to `True`.
    ticks (bool, optional): Hide top and right ticks. Defaults to `True`.
    ax (axis, optional): Axis to tune. Defaults to `plt.gca()`.

functions.name: wbml.plot.style
documentation:
Generate style setting for functions in {mod}`matplotlib.pyplot`.

Args:

: name (str): Name of style.
  kind (str): Kind of setting.

Returns:

: dict: Style settings.

functions.name: wbml.plot.pdfcrop
documentation:
Run pdfcrop on a PDF.

Args:
    path (str): Path of PDF.

variables.name: wbml.plot.scheme
documentation: list[str]: Color scheme.

variables.name: wbml.plot.colour_map
documentation: dict[str, str]: Name to colour mapping.

variables.name: wbml.plot.line_style_map
documentation: dict[str, str]: Name to line style mapping.

variables.name: wbml.plot.scatter_style_map
documentation: dict[str, str]: Name to scatter style mapping.

functions.name: wbml.net.ff
documentation:
A standard feed-forward neural net.

Args:

: output_size (int): Size of output.
  widths (tuple of int): Widths of the layers.
  nonlinearity (function, optional): Nonlinearity to use. Defaults to

  > `B.relu`.

  normalise (bool, optional): Interleave with normalisation layers.

  : Defaults to `True`.

Returns:

: {class}`.net.Net`: Feed-forward NN.

functions.name: wbml.net.rnn
documentation:
A standard recurrent neural net.

Args:

: output_size (int): Size of output.
  widths (tuple of int): Widths of the layers.
  nonlinearity (function, optional): Nonlinearity to use. Defaults to

  > `B.relu`.

  normalise (bool, optional): Interleave with normalisation layers.

  : Defaults to `False`.

  gru (bool, optional): Use GRU layers instead of Elman layers. Defaults

  : to `True`.

  final_dense (bool, optional): Append a final dense layer. Defaults to

  : `False`.

Returns:

: {class}`.net.Net`: RNN.

classes.name: wbml.net.Layer

methods.name: wbml.net.Layer.initialise
documentation:
Initialise the weights of the layer.

Args:

: input_size (int): Size of the input.
  vs ({class}`varz.Vars`): Variable container.

methods.name: wbml.net.Layer.num_weights
documentation:
Get the number of weights used in the layer.

Args:
    input_size (int): Size of the input.

Returns:
    int: Number of weights.

methods.name: wbml.net.Layer.width
documentation: Width of the layer.
A layer.

classes.name: wbml.net.Normalise
documentation:
Normalisation layer.

Args:
    epsilon (scalar): Small value to add to the standard deviation before

Linear layer.

Args:
    width (int): Width of the layer.

classes.name: wbml.net.Activation
documentation:
Activation layer.

Args:
    nonlinearity (function, optional): Nonlinearity. Defaults to `B.relu`.

classes.name: wbml.net.Net

A composition of layers.

Args:

: layers ({class}`.net.Layer`): List of the layers to compose.

classes.name: wbml.net.Recurrent
documentation:
A recurrent layer.

Args:

: cell ({class}`.net.Cell`): Cell of the recurrent layer.

  > that will be learned.

Initialise the weights of the cell.

Get the number of weights used in the cell.

Args:

documentation:
A gated recurrent unit.

Args:
    width (int): Width of the hidden.
    nonlinearity (function, optional): Nonlinearity to use. Defaults to
        `B.tanh`.

classes.name: wbml.net.Elman
documentation:
Elman cell.

Args:
    width (int): Width of the layer.
    nonlinearity (function, optional): Nonlinearity to use. Defaults to

Parse data and match results.

Args:

Part of a regex.

classes.name: wbml.parser.SkipUntil
documentation:
Skip until a certain character is found. Then also skip that
character.

Args:
    char (str): Character to find.
